title: Implement a syscall safely
description: Add a system call by validating inputs at the boundary, performing the minimal privileged action, and returning proper errno codes.
body: Steps: 1. Define the ABI: argument types, sizes, and the stable contract. 2. At entry, copy and validate every argument with the user accessors and bounds-check sizes/indices. 3. Perform the minimal privileged operation, acquiring locks in a fixed order. 4. Unwind cleanly on every error path and return a precise -errno. 5. Test with valid, boundary, and malicious inputs plus fault injection. Rollback point: before changing the ABI, which user programs depend on. Validation: fault-injection and bad-pointer tests return errors without panicking, and the ABI test suite passes; a syscall that works on valid input but panics on a bad pointer is not done.
